message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker to reflect parity orchestrator and core engine work

The beads tracker entries are updated to record the completion status of the
five parity orchestrators (WAL/journal, lock/txn, maintenance, crash recovery,
concurrent writer) and the core engine expansions (connection layer, SSI
conflict model, WAL FEC pipeline tests, observability hooks). This keeps the
project's issue-tracking state in sync with the actual code changes so that
downstream planning tools and dashboards reflect accurate progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-14vp7","title":"Vectorized Execution + Morsel-Driven Parallelism (§8.2)","description":"Replace row-at-a-time VDBE interpretation with vectorized batch processing and morsel-driven parallel execution. This is scored 10.0 in the opportunity matrix — the single largest known performance uplift available.

## Background
SQLite's VDBE processes one row at a time with significant per-row interpretation overhead (opcode dispatch, type checking, memory allocation). Modern analytical engines (DuckDB, HyPer, MonetDB/X100) process data in columnar batches of 1024-4096 rows, amortizing interpretation overhead and enabling SIMD vectorization.

## Mathematical Foundation
Boncz et al. 2005 (MonetDB/X100) showed that vectorized execution achieves near-hardware-limit throughput by keeping data in CPU caches. Leis et al. 2014 (morsel-driven parallelism) showed that splitting work into cache-sized 'morsels' and dispatching to worker threads achieves near-linear scaling with cores while maintaining low scheduling overhead.

## Key Papers
- Boncz, Zukowski, Nes 2005: 'MonetDB/X100: Hyper-Pipelining Query Execution'
- Leis, Boncz, Kemper, Neumann 2014: 'Morsel-Driven Parallelism: A NUMA-Aware Query Evaluation Framework'
- Kersten, Leis, Kemper, Neumann, Pavlo 2018: 'Everything You Always Wanted to Know About Compiled and Vectorized Query Engines'

## Implementation Plan
1. Define columnar batch format (Arrow-compatible): arrays of typed values, validity bitmaps for NULLs
2. Vectorized operators: scan, filter, project, hash-join, aggregation, sort
3. SIMD primitives for filter evaluation, hash computation, comparison
4. Morsel dispatcher: partition table scans into morsels, assign to worker threads via work-stealing
5. Pipeline breakers (hash-join build, sort) use parallel partitioned algorithms

## Expected Impact
- 5-20x throughput improvement on analytical queries (scan-heavy, filter-heavy)
- Near-linear scaling with CPU cores for parallel queries
- Reduced memory bandwidth pressure through columnar layout

## Connection to Other Features
- Leapfrog Triejoin can emit vectorized batches for downstream operators
- SSI read-set tracking must handle batch-level granularity
- Buffer pool (S3-FIFO) feeds vectorized scan with page-aligned reads
- EBR epoch management aligns with morsel boundaries

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Columnar batch format defined with Arrow compatibility
- [ ] All core operators vectorized (scan, filter, join, agg, sort)
- [ ] SIMD acceleration for filter and hash operations
- [ ] Morsel-driven parallel dispatcher with work-stealing
- [ ] TPC-H queries run end-to-end through vectorized engine
- [ ] Benchmark: 5x+ improvement over interpreter on TPC-H SF1","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:38.265137567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:38.265137567Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","execution-engine","performance"]}
 {"id":"bd-14vp7.1","title":"Design vectorized batch format (columnar, 1024-row batches)","description":"Design the core columnar batch format for vectorized execution. Each batch contains 1024 rows (tunable) stored in columnar layout: one array per column. Design considerations:

1. Column types: integers (i8/i16/i32/i64), floats (f32/f64), blobs, text (offset+data), NULL bitmap
2. Memory layout: aligned arrays for SIMD (32-byte or 64-byte alignment)
3. Validity bitmap: one bit per row per column for NULL tracking
4. Selection vector: indices of 'active' rows (for filter pushdown without copying)
5. Ownership: arena-allocated per-batch, freed as a unit
6. Arrow compatibility: layout should be zero-copy convertible to/from Arrow arrays

The batch format is the fundamental data exchange unit between all vectorized operators. Getting this right is critical — it determines SIMD compatibility, cache behavior, and memory efficiency.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch format specification document
- [ ] Rust type definitions for Batch, Column, SelectionVector
- [ ] Construction from row-oriented input (for compatibility)
- [ ] Zero-copy Arrow conversion (both directions)
- [ ] Micro-benchmark: batch construction throughput
- [ ] Memory alignment verified for SIMD operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:50.057650596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:49:24.153594148Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:49:24.153572818Z","close_reason":"Implemented vectorized batch format module, row->batch builder, Arrow-compatible zero-copy interchange, alignment verification, benchmark, and spec doc","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","design","execution-engine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14vp7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-14vp7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:26Z"},{"id":1610,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.3.1 — SQL Semantic Gap Map and Closure Backlog

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/semantic_gap_map.rs` — explicit gap map from
differential failures to code locations and semantics clauses, organized by SQL
execution pipeline stage (parser, resolver, planner, codegen, execute, storage).

### Key Components

1. **`PipelineStage` enum** — 7 sequential pipeline stages (Parse, Resolve, Plan,
   Codegen, Execute, Storage, CrossCutting) with `from_subsystem()` mapping.

2. **`GapEntry`** — Single semantic gap with:
   - Unique gap ID (GAP-{hash prefix})
   - Mismatch signature for deduplication
   - Pipeline stage and subsystem attribution
   - Feature IDs from parity taxonomy (auto-linked by keyword matching)
   - Behavior contract (expected vs actual, spec reference)
   - Code locations (inferred by subsystem heuristics)
   - Severity classification (Critical/Major/Minor/Info)

3. **`GapAnalyzer`** — Analyzes MinimalReproductions against FeatureUniverse:
   - Auto-links features by SQL keyword matching against taxonomy titles
   - Infers code locations from subsystem (e.g., Parser -> fsqlite-parser)
   - Infers severity from divergence patterns (error vs result vs cosmetic)
   - Sorts by severity then pipeline stage

4. **`SemanticGapMap`** — Complete gap map with:
   - Deterministic content hash
   - Per-stage, per-subsystem, per-severity statistics
   - Affected feature ID tracking
   - JSON serializable for CI artifacts

5. **`ClosureBacklog`** — Prioritized task list generated from gap map:
   - One closure item per gap with title, description, priority
   - Behavior contract and reproduction SQL embedded in description
   - Grouped by pipeline stage

6. **`GapSeverity`** — 4-level severity with numeric priority:
   - Critical: error vs rows divergence (one engine errors, other returns data)
   - Major: rows vs rows divergence (both return data but different)
   - Minor: cosmetic differences
   - Info: detected but no impact

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 20 tests, all passing:

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:26Z"},{"id":1612,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.3.2 — Parser/Resolver/PRAGMA Closure Wave

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/closure_wave.rs` — systematic closure wave
infrastructure for identifying and closing semantic gaps in the parser, name
resolver, and PRAGMA handling pipeline stages.

### Key Components

1. **`ClosureDomain` enum** — 3 domains (Parser, Resolver, Pragma) with `pipeline_stage()`
   mapping to `PipelineStage` from semantic_gap_map.

2. **`ClosureOutcome` enum** — 4 outcomes (Pass, Fail, Skip, Error) with Display impl.

3. **`ClosureCase`** — Single closure test case with:
   - Unique case ID (e.g., PARSE-001, RESOLVE-001, PRAGMA-001)
   - Domain classification and feature tags
   - Schema SQL (setup) and workload SQL (test)
   - Expected behavior description
   - Outcome tracking with optional failure detail
   - Spec section reference

4. **`ClosureWaveRegistry`** — Registry of closure cases with:
   - Domain filtering
   - Outcome counting
   - Coverage report generation with deterministic SHA-256 hash

5. **`build_canonical_catalog()`** — 50 canonical closure cases:
   - **20 parser cases** (PARSE-001..020): literals, aliases, INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE,
     DDL, expressions (CASE, BETWEEN, IN, LIKE, GLOB), compound SELECTs, subqueries
   - **15 resolver cases** (RESOLVE-001..015): column resolution, table-qualified refs,
     aliases, JOINs, ambiguity errors, scope isolation, ORDER BY/GROUP BY, star expansion
   - **15 PRAGMA cases** (PRAGMA-001..015): table_info, database_list, compile_options,
     journal_mode, encoding, page_size, cache_size, integrity_check, foreign_keys,
     user_version, application_id, index_list, index_info, quick_check, collation_list

6. **`ClosureCoverageReport`** — Coverage report with:
   - Per-domain statistics (total, passed, failed, skipped, closure rate)
   - Overall closure rate
   - Deterministic content hash for CI artifact tracking
   - JSON serialization/deserialization
   - Human-readable summary line

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 16 tests, all passing:

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:27Z"},{"id":1614,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.3.3 — Planner + VDBE Opcode Semantic Closure Wave

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/planner_vdbe_closure.rs` — systematic closure
wave infrastructure for identifying and closing behavioral deltas in the query
planner and VDBE execution engine.

### Key Components

1. **`PlannerVdbeDomain` enum** — 2 domains (Planner, Vdbe) mapping to
   `PipelineStage::Plan` and `PipelineStage::Execute` respectively.

2. **`PlannerVdbeCase`** — Closure case specific to planner/VDBE with:
   - Unique case IDs (PLAN-nnn, VDBE-nnn)
   - Domain classification and feature tags
   - Schema SQL setup and workload SQL
   - Expected behavior description and outcome tracking

3. **`PlannerVdbeRegistry`** — Registry with domain filtering, outcome counting,
   and coverage report generation with deterministic SHA-256 hash.

4. **`build_planner_vdbe_catalog()`** — 50 canonical closure cases:

   **20 Planner cases** covering:
   - Access path selection: full scan, rowid lookup, index equality/range, non-indexed filter (PLAN-001..005)
   - Join ordering: 2-table equijoin, 3-table chain, CROSS JOIN, self-join, LEFT JOIN (PLAN-006..010)
   - ORDER BY planning: indexed, DESC, LIMIT, OFFSET (PLAN-011..014)
   - Aggregate planning: simple aggregates, GROUP BY, HAVING (PLAN-015..017)
   - Subquery/compound: FROM subquery, EXISTS, UNION ORDER BY (PLAN-018..020)

   **30 VDBE cases** covering:
   - Expression evaluation: integer/real arithmetic, string concat, NULL propagation (VDBE-001..004)
   - Comparison operators: standard and NULL-aware (VDBE-005..008)
   - Control flow: CASE/COALESCE/NULLIF/IIF (VDBE-009..012)
   - Type affinity: TEXT column storage, CAST expressions (VDBE-013..014)
   - Cursor operations: basic iteration, empty table, index seek (VDBE-015..017)
   - Aggregates: COUNT/SUM/AVG with NULLs, MIN/MAX mixed types, empty set, GROUP_CONCAT (VDBE-018..022)
   - Sorting: multi-column ORDER BY, DISTINCT (VDBE-023..024)
   - Data modification: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, INSERT OR REPLACE (VDBE-025..028)
   - Special ops: bitwise, BETWEEN/IN (VDBE-029..030)

5. **`PlannerVdbeCoverageReport`** — Coverage report with:
   - Per-domain statistics (total, passed, failed, skipped, closure rate)
   - Deterministic content hash for CI artifact tracking
   - JSON serialization/deserialization
   - Human-readable summary line

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 18 tests, all passing:
```
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_catalog_size ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_domain_filtering ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_all_initially_skip ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_coverage_report_all_skip ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_coverage_report_with_passes ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_coverage_per_domain ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_json_roundtrip ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_hash_deterministic ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_summary_line ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_outcome_counts ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_domain_display ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_domain_pipeline_stage ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_case_ids_unique ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_all_cases_have_workload ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_empty_registry_coverage ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_planner_case_ids_prefixed ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_vdbe_case_ids_prefixed ... ok
test planner_vdbe_closure::tests::test_schema_version ... ok
```

**Reproduction**: `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib planner_vdbe_closure`

**Quality gates**: cargo check clean (my module), cargo fmt clean, all 18 tests passing.
Pre-existing compilation errors in soak_executor.rs (added by other agents) do not affect this module.

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs` (added `pub mod planner_vdbe_closure;`)

  - KnownGap for documented limitations (autocheckpoint)

#### 5) E2E verification script

### Parity Gap Analysis
- journal_mode response: PARITY (all 6 modes)
- checkpoint modes: PARITY (PASSIVE/FULL/RESTART/TRUNCATE)
- Non-WAL sentinel (0,-1,-1): PARITY
- Mode transitions with data integrity: PARITY
- Known gap: wal_autocheckpoint stored but not enforced (does not affect query results)

All 20 golden DBs pass integrity_check, have nonzero pages, have tables, and match checksums.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bsz","depends_on_id":"bd-3cbw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bsz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2365,"issue_id":"bd-3bsz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: Validate all golden copies pass PRAGMA integrity_check

### What
Run `PRAGMA integrity_check` on every golden database to ensure we have a clean, uncorrupted baseline.

### Why this is critical
If a golden copy is already corrupt (e.g., torn during backup, or pre-existing corruption in the source), all downstream tests are invalid. This gate must pass before any other testing begins.

### Method
```bash
for db in sample_sqlite_db_files/golden/*.db; do
    result=$(sqlite3 "$db" "PRAGMA integrity_check")
    if [ "$result" != "ok" ]; then
        echo "FAIL: $db — $result"
        exit 1
    fi
done
```

### Edge cases
- Some beads.db files may have schema quirks from beads_rust. That's fine as long as integrity_check passes.
- If any database fails, investigate: was the .backup copy clean? Re-copy.
- Also run `PRAGMA quick_check` as a faster sanity check

### Also verify
- `PRAGMA page_count > 0` (not an empty database)
- `SELECT count(*) FROM sqlite_master > 0` (has at least one table)

### Acceptance Criteria
- All golden databases pass `PRAGMA integrity_check` → "ok"
- All have non-zero page counts and at least one table
- Verification script committed as part of the test suite
